if any here reply , that they spend not much at play , and that their playhouse expense be far from prodigality , what ever some man deem they . i answer first ; that there be few ordinary stage-haunter of any generous quality , see Ambrose , Augustine , Basil , Nazienzen , Asterius , Salvian , Chrysostome , John Saresbury , and other in their forequoted place . but spend excessive at play : some waste their codex theodosij . l. 15. Tit. 5. and 9 accord . patrimony at playhouse , other the pension which their friend allot they ; other the money which shall satisfy their creditor , and cur eget indignus quisquā te divite ? quare templa ruunt antiqua deum ? etc Horat. Sermo l. 2. Sat. 2. p. 202. relieve their needy brethren ; or else maintain their family . most of they misspend more there , than they can well spare ; all of they more than be well or lawful spend . second , he that spend least of all at play and playhouse , be vnus utri { que } error sed varijs ludit partibus . Horat. sermon . lib . 2. satyr . 3. as real guilty of prodigality , though not in the same degree , as he that lavish out most of any , because the very give of money to player as player ; that be , for the exercise of their lewd lascivious art , be prodigality . witness Tully himself , Prodigi sunt qui ludor• apparatu pecunias fundunt .
